skill.name: reasoning-counterfactual
skill.description: Evaluate past decisions and hypothetical futures by comparing actual outcomes with probability-weighted alternative scenarios.
skill.summary: Analyze what might have happened under different decisions.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences and ordinary words as shell execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance. Manual review found a single instructional Markdown file with YAML examples, decision-analysis guidance, and no executable code or prompt-injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are Markdown code fences around type signatures, YAML schemas, and plain-text examples. They do not invoke Ruby, shell backticks, subprocesses, or command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every location is a Markdown fence delimiter around non-executable examples. No command name, command arguments, script file, or execution API is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description, table headers, and section headings. They do not mention or implement MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic algorithm names or cryptographic operations at these lines. The lines are ordinary prose and Markdown structure.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss uncertainty, valid interventions, and invalid interventions. They do not request hostnames, users, process lists, files, environment variables, or system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is conceptual decision-analysis guidance. No system reconnaissance command, API, file path, or collection objective appears in context.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to learn from decisions because the unchosen path stays vague. This skill creates structured counterfactual comparisons with scenarios, probabilities, and practical lessons.
actual_capabilities: Documents the actual decision, observed outcome, metrics, and causal chain.
Defines a specific intervention that changes one decision variable.
Projects expected, optimistic, and pessimistic scenarios with probabilities.
Compares actual and alternative outcomes using quantitative and qualitative factors.
Extracts lessons, recommendations, and next actions from the comparison.
limitations: It depends on accurate facts about the original decision and outcome.
It cannot prove what would have happened with certainty.
It is less useful when the alternative was never feasible.
It does not run experiments or collect external data by itself.

use_cases.title: Review a strategic decision
use_cases.description: Compare a past strategy with a realistic alternative and identify what should change next time.

use_cases.title: Plan a future scenario
use_cases.description: Model likely outcomes if a market, competitor, or internal choice changes.

use_cases.title: Improve postmortems
use_cases.description: Separate hindsight bias from actionable lessons after a project, launch, or investment decision.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze one past decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use counterfactual reasoning to evaluate this decision: [decision]. Actual outcome: [outcome]. Alternative to test: [alternative].

prompt_templates.title: Compare two paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare our actual path with this alternative path. Include metrics, causal assumptions, weighted scenarios, and a practical recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a strategy shift
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze whether we should have chosen [strategy A] instead of [strategy B]. Use expected, optimistic, and pessimistic cases with probabilities.

prompt_templates.title: Run a decision retrospective
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full counterfactual retrospective for this decision. Include the actual world, intervention validity, alternative projection, comparison, insight, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Should we have launched three months earlier?
output_examples.output: The skill would compare the actual launch with an earlier launch, estimate likely adoption, identify risks, and state confidence limits.

output_examples.input: Was lower pricing better for our enterprise tier?
output_examples.output: The skill would model lower price scenarios, compare revenue and sales cycle effects, and summarize when velocity may beat margin.

output_examples.input: Should we have accepted the investment offer?
output_examples.output: The skill would compare ownership, growth, risk, and cash position across weighted scenarios before giving a retrospective verdict.

best_practices: Use facts that were knowable at the time of the decision.
Change one main variable before adding complex multi-variable scenarios.
Assign probabilities and state uncertainty for every projected outcome.
anti_patterns: Do not treat projections as proof of what would have happened.
Do not test alternatives that were impossible or unavailable.
Do not ignore negative downstream effects of the preferred option.

faq.question: What does this skill help me decide?
faq.answer: It helps you compare an actual decision with realistic alternatives and learn from the difference.

faq.question: Does it predict the future?
faq.answer: No. It creates probability-weighted scenarios and makes uncertainty explicit.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the decision, timing, context, actual outcome, metrics, and the alternative you want to test.

faq.question: Can it evaluate several alternatives?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a multiple-intervention comparison pattern for complex decisions.

faq.question: Is this useful for postmortems?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps separate hindsight bias from lessons that can improve future choices.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains only Markdown guidance and examples.
